deep_dive.title: FoggyTrust: Robust Federated Learning with Hierarchical Trust Networks
deep_dive.abstract: Byzantine-robust federated learning seeks to protect distributed model training from malicious or corrupted clients without requiring access to their private data. FLTrust addresses this challenge by introducing a trusted server-side root dataset that assigns trust scores to client updates for more robust aggregation. In this work, we propose FOGGYTRUST, a hierarchical extension of FLTrust that localizes trust computation to fog nodes, allowing the framework to better handle globally heterogeneous data while preserving robustness within locally homogeneous client groups. We further show that this two-level architecture can simultaneously address distribution mismatch in trust estimation and client drift across groups by combining local trust-based aggregation with heterogeneity-aware global optimizers such as FedAdam and SCAFFOLD. Across benchmark datasets, FOGGYTRUST achieves its strongest gains on more challenging heterogeneous settings, particularly on CIFAR-10 under Krum and Trim attacks, where it achieves an over 50% improvement over FLTrust. We also test FOGGYTRUST in a real-world safari dataset to show the promise of hierarchical trust networks for robust federated learning in socially impactful, safety-critical settings such as distributed wildlife monitoring.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: directly addresses privacy in distributed ML via robust federated learning
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 直接涉及分布式机器学习中的隐私保护，通过鲁棒联邦学习
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 直接涉及分布式机器学习中的隐私保护，通过鲁棒联邦学习
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 直接涉及分布式机器学习中的隐私保护，通过鲁棒联邦学习
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: FoggyTrust proposes a hierarchical trust network for Byzantine-robust federated learning, localizing trust computation to fog nodes to handle heterogeneous data while preserving privacy.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 联邦学习面临拜占庭攻击和数据异构挑战，现有方法依赖全局信任评分。本文提出FoggyTrust，一种层次化信任网络扩展，将信任计算本地化到雾节点，在保持局部同质组内鲁棒性的同时处理全局异构数据。实验表明，该架构能同时缓解信任估计中的分布偏移和客户端漂移，为分布式机器学习提供了更健壮且隐私友好的聚合方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 联邦学习面临拜占庭攻击和数据异构挑战，现有方法依赖全局信任评分。本文提出FoggyTrust，一种层次化信任网络扩展，将信任计算本地化到雾节点，在保持局部同质组内鲁棒性的同时处理全局异构数据。实验表明，该架构能同时缓解信任估计中的分布偏移和客户端漂移，为分布式机器学习提供了更健壮且隐私友好的聚合方案。

deep_dive.title: Productionized Fairness Measurement Under Privacy Constraints
deep_dive.abstract: Fairness measurements in the form of disaggregated evaluations often rely on demographic signals that are legally constrained or culturally sensitive. Race and ethnicity signals are among the more difficult signals to curate and use for this task. This paper presents Privacy-Preserving Probabilistic Race/Ethnicity Estimation (PPRE) as a method for enabling fairness measurements with respect to race/ethnicity for U.S.\ LinkedIn members in a privacy-preserving manner. PPRE applies privacy technologies (specifically: secure two-party computation, differential privacy, and additive homomorphic encryption) on top of two race/ethnicity demographic signal sources (the Bayesian Improved Surname Geocoding estimator and a sparse golden survey set of self-reported demographics) to power a fairness measurement solution with respect to US-based race/ethnicity demographics. We detail its privacy guarantees and demonstrate its application on candidate- and viewer-side fairness measurements. We close with a transferable framework for institutions seeking to implement similar privacy-preserving measurement infrastructure.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: privacy-preserving fairness measurement with differential privacy
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 使用差分隐私的公平性测量
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 使用差分隐私的公平性测量
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 使用差分隐私的公平性测量
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes PPRE, a privacy-preserving method for race/ethnicity estimation using secure computation and differential privacy.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出PPRE方法，在隐私约束下实现种族/族裔的公平性测量。结合贝叶斯改进姓氏地理编码和稀疏自我报告数据，利用安全两方计算、差分隐私和加法同态加密，确保在不泄露敏感人口统计信息的前提下进行公平性评估。实验表明该方法在LinkedIn数据上有效。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该论文提出PPRE方法，在隐私约束下实现种族/族裔的公平性测量。结合贝叶斯改进姓氏地理编码和稀疏自我报告数据，利用安全两方计算、差分隐私和加法同态加密，确保在不泄露敏感人口统计信息的前提下进行公平性评估。实验表明该方法在LinkedIn数据上有效。

deep_dive.title: Differential Privacy over Hamming Codes
deep_dive.abstract: We consider the transmission of the outputs of counting queries over a binary symmetric channel (BSC), where Hamming codes are employed as the channel encoder. Since the channel is inherently noisy, this transmission already provides a degree of privacy protection ``for free'', albeit at the cost of reduced utility in the form of decoding errors. A natural question is whether this privacy can be further improved (i) without any additional real-time obfuscation of the data, such as injecting artificial noise prior to transmission, and (ii) without increasing the end-to-end error probability. In this work, we answer this question in the affirmative by deriving an optimal codeword arrangement that strictly improves differential privacy guarantees while incurring no real-time computational overhead and no degradation in utility.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 基于汉明码的差分隐私
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 基于汉明码的差分隐私
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 基于汉明码的差分隐私
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Shows that optimal codeword arrangement in Hamming-coded transmission improves differential privacy without extra noise or increased error probability.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文研究在汉明码传输通道中利用已有噪声实现差分隐私，通过优化码字排列在不增加实时噪声和误码率的前提下提升差分隐私保证。理论推导了最优排列方案，并证明其严格优于随机排列。该方法无需额外计算开销即可获得隐私增益，适用于需要低延迟通信的分布式场景。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该论文研究在汉明码传输通道中利用已有噪声实现差分隐私，通过优化码字排列在不增加实时噪声和误码率的前提下提升差分隐私保证。理论推导了最优排列方案，并证明其严格优于随机排列。该方法无需额外计算开销即可获得隐私增益，适用于需要低延迟通信的分布式场景。

quick_skim.title: Ask, Don't Judge: Binary Questions for Interpretable LLM Evaluation and Self-Improvement
quick_skim.abstract: Evaluating LLM outputs remains a major bottleneck in NLP: human evaluation is expensive and slow, lexical metrics correlate poorly with human judgments on open-ended generation, and holistic LLM judges often produce opaque scores that are hard to debug. We propose BINEVAL, a framework that decomposes evaluation criteria into atomic binary questions and aggregates the resulting verdicts into interpretable, multi-dimensional scores. Given a task prompt, a meta-prompt generates fine-grained evaluation questions, and an LLM answers them independently for each output, yielding transparent question-level feedback together with calibrated overall scores. This decomposition makes evaluation easier to inspect, easier to diagnose, and directly usable for prompt improvement. Across SummEval, Topical-Chat, and QAGS, BINEVAL matches or outperforms strong baselines including UniEval and G-Eval, with especially strong results on factual consistency benchmarks such as QAGS. Beyond competitive correlation with human judgments, BINEVAL better matches human score distributions and avoids the ceiling effects common in prior LLM judges, leading to better discrimination between borderline and clearly flawed outputs. We further show that the same question-level feedback supports iterative prompt optimization, improving evaluator prompts on summarization and generation prompts on IFBench under both self-update and cross-model update settings. Overall, BINEVAL provides a task-agnostic, training-free, and interpretable evaluation framework that combines strong empirical performance with practical diagnostic and optimization value.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: LLM evaluation decomposition into binary questions
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: LLM评估分解为二进制问题
quick_skim.llm_evidence: LLM评估分解为二进制问题
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: LLM评估分解为二进制问题
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes BINEVAL framework that decomposes evaluation criteria into atomic binary questions for interpretable LLM evaluation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对LLM评估中评分不透明和调试困难的问题，提出BINEVAL框架，将评估标准分解为原子二进制问题，由LLM独立回答并聚合为多维度分数，使评估过程透明且可诊断。实验表明该方法在多个任务上提高了评估的可解释性和可靠性。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文针对LLM评估中评分不透明和调试困难的问题，提出BINEVAL框架，将评估标准分解为原子二进制问题，由LLM独立回答并聚合为多维度分数，使评估过程透明且可诊断。实验表明该方法在多个任务上提高了评估的可解释性和可靠性。

quick_skim.title: Reliable Homomorphic Matching for Fuzzy Labeled PSI at Scale
quick_skim.abstract: Fuzzy Labeled Private Set Intersection (FLPSI) lets a receiver learn the labels of enrolled records similar to its query, and nothing else. Constructions based on a set-threshold reduction reach practical performance: a query matches a record when the two agree on a threshold number of components, and the private matching is delegated to an inner set-threshold kernel. We study its homomorphic form, which combines leveled-BFV homomorphic encryption (HE), a garbled circuit, and secret sharing to decide the match under encryption and release the record's label. We identify a composition gap in this kernel: efficiency is bought with a per-trial false-accept probability, but one query runs a trial for every record, so the error compounds with the database size into the kernel's realization soundness error (RSE), the rate at which it accepts a query the plaintext matcher would reject. The RSE is a reliability property of the cryptographic matching layer, not the matcher's accuracy, and a sound kernel must contribute zero or negligible RSE of its own. We formalize this as a composable security property, give a closed-form bound on the receiver's advantage, and close the gap with CSTPSI, a kernel that runs independent token rounds and raises the per-trial bound to a matching power. We prove CSTPSI secure in the semi-honest model. The bound sets the round count: two token rounds suffice for million-scale databases and three for billion-scale at the $10^{-6}$ engineering threshold. Our evaluation confirms this: at a million records the baseline kernel's RSE reaches 100% while CSTPSI holds it at 0 in every measured configuration. For large labels at small to moderate scale CSTPSI is more than 20x faster than the baseline, with up to 93% less communication, converging to the baseline only at million-scale. Our implementation, with a one-command reproducibility harness, is publicly available.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Homomorphic matching for fuzzy labeled private set intersection
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于同态加密的模糊标签隐私集合交集
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于同态加密的模糊标签隐私集合交集
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于同态加密的模糊标签隐私集合交集
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Presents reliable homomorphic matching for FLPSI using BFV, garbled circuits, and secret sharing, addressing error accumulation in large databases
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对大规模模糊标签隐私集合交集（FLPSI）中的错误累积问题，提出可靠的同态匹配方案。该方案结合级联BFV同态加密、混淆电路和秘密共享，在保证隐私的同时实现了可扩展性。实验表明，该方法在百万级数据库上保持低误报率，为分布式场景下的隐私数据匹配提供了实用方案。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文针对大规模模糊标签隐私集合交集（FLPSI）中的错误累积问题，提出可靠的同态匹配方案。该方案结合级联BFV同态加密、混淆电路和秘密共享，在保证隐私的同时实现了可扩展性。实验表明，该方法在百万级数据库上保持低误报率，为分布式场景下的隐私数据匹配提供了实用方案。
